statement: Do not let a generated summary note silently replace source-preserving context where source integrity materially matters.
what_it_prevents: Lossy summaries becoming surrogate source context.

statement: Do not separate explanation from the explained text blindly.
what_it_prevents: Orphaned explanations, hunting, and version-mismatch corruption.

statement: Do not rely on a book’s loose proof rendering as the only authoritative proof layer.
what_it_prevents: Memorizing or teaching unconfirmed proof renderings as final source truth.

statement: Do not discard book-preserved proof excerpts just because authoritative fetching exists.
what_it_prevents: Loss of the scholar’s actual cited wording and local reasoning context.

statement: Do not assume all scholar methodologies fit prewritten rules and protocols.
what_it_prevents: False completeness and silent failure on unseen patterns.

statement: Do not treat the note question as the whole issue when the pair is structurally malformed before the note enters the picture.
what_it_prevents: Superficial UX fixes masking deeper excerpt corruption.

statement: Do not treat a readable note-backed explanation as proven safe if the explanation-to-proof linkage is still unresolved.
what_it_prevents: False confidence from readability without integrity.

statement: Do not collapse all hadith explanations into one generic proof source when version, wording, or grading may differ.
Do not proceed with confident automated separation when the system cannot determine the methodology fit with high certainty.
what_it_prevents: Silent corruption under unknown edge cases.

statement: Do not optimize for neat separation-of-concerns at the expense of knowledge integrity.
what_it_prevents: Clean architecture that produces unsafe scholarly units.
